items.title: Netflix Wiz creates app to slash AI bills, then open sources it
items.why_it_matters: A Netflix engineer built and open-sourced an app to reduce AI API costs. This shows someone shipping a practical tool that directly addresses your interest in saving money on AI tokens, similar to your past positive feedback on cost savings. Explore the open-source repository to see how they achieved cost savings and consider if you can adapt any of their strategies or code for your own projects this week.

items.title: Show HN: Agents, run any coding agent on your subscription not API costs
items.why_it_matters: A new open-source CLI tool allows you to run coding agents using your existing subscriptions, potentially saving on API costs. This directly addresses your goal of reducing AI costs and leveraging different models efficiently, aligning with your past positive feedback on token savings. Install the `agents-cli.sh` tool and experiment with running Claude Code or Codex through it to see if you can reduce your API expenses this week.

items.title: With Claude: Less Coding, More Testing
items.why_it_matters: An article discusses how to shift focus from writing code to testing more effectively when using AI tools like Claude. This directly supports your goal of improving the 'direction' part of vibe coding, helping you focus on verification and quality rather than just generating code. Read the article for specific strategies and apply one of the suggested testing approaches to your current AI-ingest project this week.

items.title: Show HN: Ralphy – open-source autonomous Claude Codd built on the Ralph loop
items.why_it_matters: An open-source tool called Ralphy allows Claude Code to run autonomously through tasks overnight, committing changes to a branch. This is a shipping story that offers a way to automate coding tasks with Claude, potentially speeding up your development cycle and helping you ship faster by getting work done while you're away. Clone the Ralphy repository, set it up with your Claude subscription, and try queuing a small, well-defined coding task for it to complete overnight this week.

items.title: Show HN: Ouijit, an open-source task and terminal manager for coding agents
items.why_it_matters: Someone shipped an open-source terminal manager specifically designed for coding agents like Claude and Codex, featuring task management and hooks. This is a shipping story from an indie developer, offering a tool that could significantly streamline your workflow for directing AI agents and managing project tasks. Download and set up Ouijit to manage your tasks and terminal sessions for your AI-ingest project this week, especially using its kanban board features.

items.title: What we built in 2 weeks zerostack – the lightweight coding agent
items.why_it_matters: A team shared a post about building a lightweight coding agent in just two weeks. This is a direct 'someone shipping' story with a clear timeframe, which can be highly motivating for your goal of shipping faster and learning from others' rapid development processes. Read their post to understand their development process and identify one specific technique or tool they used to ship quickly that you can apply to your AI-ingest project this week.

items.title: Claude Agent SDK: Build Your Own AI Terminal in 10 Minutes
items.why_it_matters: A guide shows how to quickly build an AI terminal using the Claude Agent SDK. This offers a quick, concrete project idea that uses a tool you're familiar with (Claude) and directly applies to building agent-based interfaces, enhancing your 'direction' skills. Follow the guide to build your own basic AI terminal with the Claude Agent SDK and integrate a simple command relevant to your AI-ingest project this week.

items.title: Agent-stack – one command to make any repo token-efficient for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: A new open-source tool, Agent-stack, provides a single command to optimize your repository for token efficiency when using Claude Code. This directly addresses your interest in cost savings and efficient use of Claude, offering a practical way to manage token usage in your projects. Install Agent-stack and run its command on your AI-ingest repository to see how it optimizes for token efficiency with Claude Code this week.

items.title: A zot extension that makes answering your coding agent's questions painless
items.why_it_matters: A new extension for Zot aims to simplify and speed up your responses to coding agents' questions. This tool directly improves the efficiency of your 'direction' workflow, making interactions with Claude Code or Codex smoother and less time-consuming. Install the Zot extension and use it during your next coding session with Claude Code to see if it makes answering agent questions more efficient this week.

items.title: Claude Code and Codex Can Have Real-Time Conversation via Git
items.why_it_matters: A method or tool is presented that enables Claude Code and Codex to have real-time conversations using Git as the communication layer. This is highly relevant to your current stack (Claude Code, Codex, Git) and could significantly enhance your ability to direct agents through a familiar version control system. Read the article to understand the approach and attempt to set up a basic real-time conversation between Claude Code and Codex in a new Git repository this week.

items.title: Headroom compresses everything your AI agent reads before it reaches the LLM
items.why_it_matters: Headroom is a new Python package that compresses input for AI agents, potentially reducing token usage and costs. This directly aligns with your interest in token efficiency and cost savings, offering a plug-and-play solution to optimize your agent interactions. Install the `headroom-ai` package and integrate it into a small part of your AI-ingest project where an agent reads a lot of context, then compare token usage this week.

items.title: Show HN: Agentpack – isolated config layers for Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode
items.why_it_matters: Someone shipped Agentpack, an open-source tool for managing isolated configuration layers for various coding agents, including Claude Code and Codex. This is a shipping story that offers a practical way to organize and manage the settings for your different AI coding tools, which can simplify your project setup. Explore the Agentpack repository and try setting up a basic configuration layer for Claude Code for a new feature you're planning for AI-ingest this week.

items.title: Show HN: Free cloud-based tool for managing AI agents across multiple hosts
items.why_it_matters: A new free, cloud-based tool for managing AI agents across different environments has been shipped. This is a shipping story that could simplify managing your AI agents, especially as you start to use them in more complex ways or across different parts of your project. Sign up for the free cloud tool and try connecting your Claude Code environment to it to explore its agent management features this week.

items.title: HarnessKit – Manage skills/MCP/hooks/plugins/memory across all your Agents
items.why_it_matters: HarnessKit is an open-source project designed to help manage various aspects like skills, plugins, and memory for multiple AI agents. As you work with Claude Code and Codex, this tool could provide a structured way to enhance and coordinate their capabilities, improving your overall direction. Clone the HarnessKit repository and experiment with defining a simple 'skill' for one of your agents that you can then manage through the kit this week.

items.title: Claude Code OS: self-updating operational memory for Claude Code (open source)
items.why_it_matters: An open-source project, Claude Code OS, aims to provide self-updating operational memory for Claude Code. This could significantly improve Claude Code's ability to retain context and learn from past interactions within your projects, making your 'direction' more effective over time. Explore the Claude Code OS GitHub repository and try integrating its memory features into a small, isolated part of your AI-ingest project this week.
